codeReview.summary: The feature commit adds the missing `/reset-password` route, registers it in the TanStack route tree, and covers the required UI states for missing-token, validation, loading, success, and error handling. I found two non-blocking concerns: the invalid-token copy still depends on backend wording, and the new tests mirror the component logic instead of exercising the real route component.

issues.description: The invalid/expired-token branch renders `resetError.message` directly. That means the page only shows friendly copy if Better Auth happens to return friendly wording; a technical or unstable backend message would leak straight into the UI instead of the stable user-facing message required by the feature contract. Normalizing this branch to a fixed friendly message would make the behavior more robust.

The new test file uses an inline `ResetPasswordPageDirect` replica instead of importing and rendering the real `packages/web/app/routes/reset-password.tsx` component. As a result, the automated coverage does not actually exercise the production route's `window.location.search` token handling or its TanStack Router wiring, so regressions in the real component could slip past the test suite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true even though the reviewed session did not follow the current `web-worker` procedure: it created the route before creating tests, and it skipped the required manual `agent-browser` verification step. Either adherence reporting needs to be stricter or the skill should explicitly describe when exceptions are acceptable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/web-worker/SKILL.md lines 24-26 require tests-first and lines 41-47 require manual verification with agent-browser; the transcript skeleton for worker session `82f7d4ab-02b0-4305-943d-90b4f8aeaf40` shows `Create` of `packages/web/app/routes/reset-password.tsx` before `Create` of `packages/web/app/__tests__/routes/reset-password.test.tsx`, and no browser-tool invocation appears before the handoff still reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared docs mention TanStack's file-based routes, but they do not mention that adding a new route also requires updating or regenerating the committed `packages/web/app/routeTree.gen.ts` artifact. The worker only discovered that requirement after typecheck and then manually edited the generated file, so this route-registration workflow should be documented in shared state.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md line 37 and `.factory/library/architecture.md` lines 23 and 40 describe file-based routing but do not mention `routeTree.gen.ts`; in the transcript skeleton, after typecheck the worker reads `packages/web/app/routeTree.gen.ts`, says it needs to update that file for the new route, and performs a series of manual edits before re-running typecheck.

summary: Reviewed feature `reset-password-page` via mission metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `d0a7001978c2213381ae92c64818a692fb455e72`. Result: pass, with two non-blocking review notes (friendly-error robustness and test realism) plus shared-state observations about web-worker procedure adherence and undocumented TanStack route-tree workflow.
